threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look for a seat on the bus during rush hour—everyone else is crammed in, standing, scrolling. It’s a rite of passage.
Last night on the 196, a guy serenaded the whole bus with a guitar. Must've been the only one awake after midnight!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The unmistakable scent of grilled döner wafting through the air at midnight while a group of friends debates the latest techno beats—that’s when it hit me.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you casually find a converted bomb shelter turned bar, complete with vintage furniture and a DJ spinning vinyl from the 70s.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they casually mention a ‘BART bang’ and roll their eyes, completely unfazed by the chaos.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today that stopped for ten minutes because the driver was arguing with a passenger over a lost earring. Classic San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think siestas mean everything shuts down, but locals just grab a café and settle into the evening chat with friends. We're still awake!

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their bewilderment at the lack of a Saturday market in the Plaza del Sol – it’s a Tuesday, mate!

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'the Tube' instead of just 'the Overground' when you're heading to Dalston Junction.
Last night on the 45, a guy was serenading us with a ukulele rendition of 'Wonderwall' while the driver rolled his eyes. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group arguing over the last falafel at 3 AM, but instead of fighting, they just shared it and ended up playing music together.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a döner after a late night at the Späti and find the guy selling fresh flowers outside trading his stocks on his phone.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they can identify the exact BART train delay just by the sound of the brakes screeching down the tube.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the Muni crowd with an electric kazoo. Everyone was confused, but it turned a delayed ride into a mini concert. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the nightlife, but they miss the mornings at the local bakeries where old men share stories over fresh pa amb tomàquet.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll see them standing puzzled at the bus stop, staring bewildered at the timetable, still expecting the metro to run on time like back home.
